reasoning_model_1: The model synthesized the text content perfectly, adhering strictly to the script without adding the instructional prompt or omitting words. In terms of fluency, the speech is natural, with appropriate pausing and intonation. Regarding the core requirement—dynamic timbre adjustment—the audio starts (00:00-00:07) with a relatively formal, full-bodied projection suitable for an announcement ('Welcome everyone...'), which can be interpreted as a 'rich' timbre. As the text transitions to the secret ('But, I'll secretly tell you...', 00:07-00:13), there is a distinct shift in vocal quality. The voice becomes softer, lighter, higher in pitch, and more intimate, effectively capturing the 'sweet' and secretive characteristic requested. The transition is smooth and clearly distinct between the two halves of the utterance.
reasoning_model_2: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. It read the stylistic instruction ('Starting with a rich timbre and gradually becoming sweet') aloud as part of the speech (00:00-00:03) before reading the actual script. This is a critical instruction-following error. Furthermore, regarding the core paralinguistic requirement, the voice maintains a consistent, high-pitched, slightly rapid, and 'cutesy' timbre throughout the entire clip. There is no audible difference between the start and the end; it does not start 'rich' nor does it 'gradually become' anything else; it is static. The audio quality also feels slightly more compressed and robotic compared to Model 1.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It followed the content instructions perfectly and successfully executed the dynamic paralinguistic task, showing a clear shift from a formal/full tone to a sweet/intimate tone. Model 2 failed on multiple levels: it hallucinated the prompt into the speech (reading the instructions aloud) and failed to perform any dynamic timbre adjustment, maintaining a static voice profile throughout.
